actions.text: Amendment SA 5336 not agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 48 - 50. Record Vote Number: 103.

Amendment SA 5336 proposed by Senator Wyden. To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to requiring the Comptroller General of the United States to conduct a study related to economic consequences of private or confidential drug pricing agreements struck by any Federal department, agency, or office with any pharmaceutical manufacturer.
Senate amendment proposed (on the floor): Amendment SA 5336 proposed by Senator Wyden.
Senate amendment not agreed to: Amendment SA 5336 not agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 48 - 50. Record Vote Number: 103.
Roll call votes on amendments in Senate: Amendment SA 5336 not agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 48 - 50. Record Vote Number: 103.

purpose: To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to requiring the Comptroller General of the United States to conduct a study related to economic consequences of private or confidential drug pricing agreements struck by any Federal department, agency, or office with any pharmaceutical manufacturer.
